regime.note: Vol compressed overnight (VIX 18.4→16.13, −2.27) into a calm/positive-gamma backdrop, but the tape is de-risking under the surface: leadership has broadened OUT of tech into defensives, financials and rate-sensitives while Tech slid one RRG quadrant (Weakening→Lagging) and its 3-month relative edge decayed (+26.4→+17.4). Calm index, rotating internals. Two-sided into the open.

attention.title: Tech dropped into the Lagging quadrant; rotation is broadening, not narrowing
attention.what: XLK RRG Weakening→Lagging (1m RS −6.2, still the highest 3m RS +17.4 but decaying from +26.4). 8 of 11 sectors now Leading on 1m RS — healthcare (+9.6), financials (+8.8), utilities (+5.7), staples (+5.1) out front. Est. from sector-member bars.
attention.why: The 3-month-leader-into-Lagging path is the classic peaking sequence: money leaving high-multiple tech across the board, not into a single defensive pocket. Confirms the QQQ/VGT/SPMO complex is swimming against the sector tide.

attention.title: Momentum factor cracking short-term while the 63-day trend still holds
attention.what: 5-day: SPMO −5.8, VGT −2.1, QQQ −2.0 (all negative); yet 63-day still strong: SPMO +29.4, VGT +27.6, QQQ +20.7. 21-day is rolling over (SPMO −2.1, QQQ −4.1, VGT −7.0). Momentum Δ = deceleration. Est. from watchlist bars.
attention.why: A short-term factor rollover on top of a still-positive long trend is the setup that either mean-reverts up or begins a trend-break — SPMO/VGT/QQQ move together, so one factor unwind hits all three at once. Watch for 5d→21d confirmation.

attention.title: Vol compressed overnight — calm/positive-gamma open, but below the flip-alert bar
attention.what: VIX 18.4→16.13 (−2.27), inside the ±3 regimeChangeVixJump threshold so no regime-flip signal; regime score flat at 0 (neutral). Term structure not fetched — assume mild contango, confirm live.
attention.why: Lower VIX = a calmer, likely positive-gamma grind at the open (dealers dampen moves), which coexists with — and can mask — the internal rotation. Calm surface is not the same as risk-off internals resolving.

attention.title: MSFT: a +5.5% 5-day pop against a −9.2% 21-day roll into the Jul-29 print
attention.what: MSFT $388.84; mom5 +5.5 (the only positive 5-day in the watchlist) but mom21 flipped +6.0 (Jul-6)→−9.2 and mom63 only +4.5. A diverged laggard bouncing short-term into a binary earnings catalyst 21 days out. Est. from bars.
attention.why: The short-term bounce vs deteriorating medium-term trend frames it as a counter-trend pop, not a trend-turn — and the Jul-29 margin/capex print is the real fork (re-rate vs retest). An event trade, not a momentum long.

attention.title: Macro-heavy week ahead: FOMC minutes TODAY, June CPI Jul 14 is the fork
attention.what: Verified calendar: FOMC minutes today 2:00pm ET + 10y auction 1:00pm; jobless claims + 30y auction Jul 9; June CPI Jul 14 8:30am; June PPI Jul 15; monthly OPEX Jul 17. 10y yield reportedly >4.5% into supply (label: recent, verify live).
attention.why: The rotation-out-of-tech thesis is rate-driven — a hot CPI (Jul 14) or hawkish minutes tone re-prices the front end and pressures long-duration tech further; a cool print is the main bull catalyst to un-lag XLK. This is the near-term decision cluster, not the far-out marquee.

attention.title: SPY levels to watch at the open — carried from Jul-6, confirm live before sizing
attention.what: Last-known SPY structure (2026-07-06, STALE): flip ~740, call wall ~750, put wall ~720, ATH 760.4, IV-rank ~15. No fresh option chain fetched this run.
attention.why: Into the open the flip is the calm/violent line: above it dealers sell strength/buy weakness (grind), below it flips to a negative-gamma acceleration. These are 2-day-old levels — re-pull the chain in the gamma lab before acting.

attention.title: Flow proxies point to a defensive/rate-sensitive bid, not a tech bid
attention.what: Proxy read (sector adv/dec + relative-momentum only — no real fund-flow feed): the 1m-RS leaders are XLV/XLF/XLU/XLP; tech/comms/energy lag. Directionally consistent with rotation-driven positioning, not fresh tech accumulation.
attention.why: Labeled a FLOW PROXY, not a flow. Useful as a tell that the broadening is defensively/financially led; a genuine tech re-accumulation would show tech relative-strength turning up first (it hasn't).

recommendations.subject: Favor XLF + XLV over XLK; trim the concentrated tech tail (VGT / SPMO) into strength
recommendations.rationale: The pair trade the tape is handing you: XLK is now formally Lagging (1m RS -6.2, 3m edge decaying +26.4->+17.4) while XLF (1m RS +8.8, RRG Leading) and XLV (1m RS +9.6, Leading) lead 8/11 sectors. Overweight XLF/XLV vs XLK until XLK's 1m RS-momentum crosses back above zero (the negate). VGT/SPMO carry the de-gross risk and printed the weakest 5-day. Estimate from RRG + momentum-Delta, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Momentum-factor 5d->21d rollover (SPMO / VGT / QQQ) — trigger to trim, not add
recommendations.rationale: 5-day momentum turned negative across the complex (SPMO -5.8, VGT -2.1, QQQ -2.0) while 63-day still holds (+29.4 / +27.6 / +20.7). The actionable trigger: if 21-day follows the 5-day below zero (VGT already -7.0, QQQ -4.1), the factor trend is breaking — trim SPMO/VGT on that confirmation. Reclaim of QQQ's 50-day + XLK RS turning up negates. Deep-link the momentum lab for the live crossover.

recommendations.subject: Carry a cheap hedge into June CPI (Jul 14) while VIX ~16 keeps premium light
recommendations.rationale: The rotation is rate-driven; CPI Jul 14 8:30am is the near-term binary that can accelerate the tech de-rate (hot) or trigger a relief squeeze (cool). With VIX compressed to 16.1 and (assumed) contango, downside protection is relatively cheap here — size a defined hedge into the print. SPY flip (~740, STALE) is the reference line; re-pull the chain first. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Avoid/fade Energy (XLE) — deepest laggard, no rotation bid
recommendations.rationale: XLE is the worst RRG cell (1m RS -5.3, 3m RS -21.6, Lagging) — the defensive/financial rotation is NOT flowing here. It is not the place to hide from the tech unwind. A turn in crude + XLE 1m RS crossing back positive would be the earliest re-entry tell. Estimate from sector RS, not advice.

recommendations.subject: MSFT into Jul 29 (FOMC-day print)
recommendations.rationale: Counter-trend +5.5% 5-day pop into a binary margin/capex print with medium-term momentum still negative (21d -9.2) — and it lands the SAME day as the FOMC decision, doubling the volatility. Event trade, not a momentum long. Contrarian and cheap but two-sided.

psychology.read: REGIME: late-cycle, low-vol rotation — a risk-ON-under-the-hood, risk-off-in-tech tape, not a broad risk-off. NAME it: positive-gamma / calm index (VIX 16.1) masking an orderly, rate-driven de-rating of long-duration tech into defensives, financials and rate-sensitives. CROWD POSTURE: complacent at the index (low VIX, Dow at records per recent reports) but actively de-grossing the AI/mega-cap-momentum complex — a 'healthy rotation' consensus that is itself the crowded view. Reaction asymmetry still favors punishing tech misses (sell-the-news). FALSIFIERS: (1) XLK 1m RS-momentum crossing back above zero + QQQ reclaiming its 50-day would break the rotation-out thesis; (2) a VIX pop >19-20 with sectors ALSO rolling over would flip 'orderly rotation' into 'broad risk-off'; (3) a cool June CPI (Jul 14) that squeezes beaten-down tech would invalidate the 'sell every tech bounce' posture.
inputs: VIX 18.4->16.13 (vol compression; below the +/-3 flip-alert threshold)
breadth broadening: 8/11 sectors Leading on 1m RS while XLK/XLC/XLE lag (proxy)
momentum-factor 5-day rollover (SPMO -5.8, VGT -2.1, QQQ -2.0) vs still-positive 63-day
MSFT +5.5% 5-day pop vs -9.2% 21-day roll (counter-trend, not trend-turn)

EXTERNAL corroboration (recent, web-verified this run): semis-led tech selloff, Nasdaq below its 50-day, rotation into healthcare/financials/staples, VIX ~15.8-16, 10y yield >4.5% — consistent with the deterministic slice
Near-term catalyst cluster VERIFIED: FOMC minutes 7/8, CPI 7/14, PPI 7/15, OPEX 7/17 — a rate-heavy fork just ahead
Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run — sentiment band is unknown, not assumed
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: CPI (Jul 14) scenario odds shaded so hot+in-line > cool: the crowd is positioned FOR continued rotation-out, so an in-line/hot print confirms the trade while a cool print is the pain/squeeze trade. MSFT miss shaded slightly heavier than the (stale) option-implied for sell-the-news asymmetry, compounded by the Jul-29 FOMC collision. OPEX pin nudged 0.58->0.55 because CPI/PPI land three days before expiry and can knock spot off the 740 pin. All are labeled estimates seeded from stale options / unverified consensus — re-seed live.

VGT.status: $114.06; 5d −2.1, 21d −7.0 (worst 21-day), 63d +27.6. Highest-beta tech proxy; falls hardest on a de-gross and sits in the Lagging sector (XLK). The concentrated single-name tail.

MSFT.status: $388.84; 5d +5.5 (only positive 5-day) but 21d -9.2, 63d +4.5. Counter-trend bounce into the Jul-29 print — which lands the SAME day as the FOMC decision (double catalyst). Medium-term momentum still negative. Event trade, not a momentum long.

experimental.title: Momentum-factor rollover monitor (idea)
experimental.note: Auto-flag when 5-day momentum turns negative across SPMO/VGT/QQQ while 63-day stays positive, then track whether 21-day confirms lower within N sessions — the crossover this run is flagging. Not yet built; promote to a tool if recurring.

experimental.title: Rate-driven rotation gauge: XLK-vs-XLF RS overlaid on the 10y yield (idea)
experimental.note: Method: plot XLK/XLF (and XLK/XLV) relative-strength ratio against the 10y Treasury yield and its 5/20-day change; regress sector RS-momentum on d(10y) to quantify how rate-sensitive the current leadership rotation is, and flag when the correlation breaks (rotation losing its rate driver = possible tech re-entry). Inputs: XLK/XLF/XLV daily bars (have), ^TNX or 10y yield series (need to add to rlData). This run's thesis is explicitly rate-driven — no owning tool ties sector RS to yields today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Double-catalyst vol event-study: FOMC-day + mega-cap earnings collision (idea)
experimental.note: Method: event-study base rates for days when an FOMC decision and a top-10 S&P name's earnings land together (Jul 29 = FOMC + MSFT) — measure realized-vs-implied vol, average |move|, and next-day drift vs single-catalyst days. Inputs: historical FOMC dates + mega-cap earnings dates + SPX/QQQ realized vol + straddle-implied. Quantifies the compounding this run flags qualitatively. Not yet built; promote if the pattern recurs across quarters.
